notes: NVIDIA is the primary merchant GPU and platform supplier for AI clusters.
TSMC advanced packaging capacity supports leading AI accelerator production.
Micron participates in HBM and DRAM supply for AI servers.
Vertiv provides power and cooling infrastructure for high density data centers.
Broadcom supports custom silicon programs for hyperscale AI customers.
Accelerators increasingly depend on high bandwidth memory for model training and inference throughput.
Advanced packaging is needed to connect compute dies and HBM efficiently.
Hyperscaler AI capex creates downstream demand for electrical infrastructure.
Higher rack densities increase the importance of liquid and advanced thermal management.
High performance networking keeps accelerator clusters utilized.
Supermicro sells server platforms used in AI infrastructure deployments.
AMD competes with NVIDIA in AI accelerators and CPUs for data center workloads.
